covered_sentences: Mrs. Dean sat down and began her story again.
Catherine continued her friendship with the Lintons and began to live a double life.
She did not show her wild side to them.
She was polite and friendly to Mr. and Mrs. Linton.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Single panel, center-focus organization: the housekeeper seated in the foreground left anchors the present-tense narration frame, and a large cloud-shaped memory bubble rises from her direction to the upper-right occupying approximately the right half of the frame, containing the flashback of Catherine's polished behavior at the Lintons. Camera positioned at medium range, angled 30 degrees right-of-front at adult chest height, looking toward the housekeeper seated in a red upholstered chair; the ornate marble fireplace is in the background right; the cloud bubble floats to the upper-right.

The housekeeper sits in a red upholstered chair, back slightly upright, hands resting in her lap, face in three-quarter view toward the viewer with a calm narrating expression. The large cloud-shaped memory bubble to the upper-right contains a smaller scene: Catherine (aristocratic period) standing gracefully in a lighter interior setting, posture composed and contained, smiling politely with hands lightly folded at her waist — her polished social self shown to the Lintons; an adult male and female silhouette pair appear flanking her in the cloud, representing Mr. and Mrs. Linton, positioned at adult scale; Catherine's smaller stature is visible relative to them within the cloud.

Thrushcross Grange drawing room as the real-time narration setting; the housekeeper sits in a red upholstered chair in the foreground-left, the ornate marble fireplace visible in the right background, its mantelpiece at approximately her waist height when seated; deep red carpet extends to the foreground; the glass chandelier is partially visible at the upper frame edge; daylight from the high windows provides warm ambient light.

Warm daytime drawing room light; the present-tense housekeeper frame uses a slightly warmer amber tone; the cloud bubble interior is rendered in lighter airier tones distinguishing memory from the present. Overall palette: warm amber and deep red.

Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face with a calm narrating expression, dark domestic dress with white apron, tied collar; seated in red upholstered chair — chair arm at approximately her elbow height when seated, chair back top near her head top level; the fireplace mantelpiece visible in the background at approximately her waist-to-hip height when seated. Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic period) in the cloud bubble: dark brown mid-length hair neatly arranged, slender figure, light or cream-colored fine dress with delicate pleats — shown as a smaller inset figure within the cloud at approximately half the housekeeper's height in the main frame. Proportion anchor: housekeeper seated with chair arm at elbow height; cloud-bubble Catherine smaller-scale inset.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

Character appearance, clothing details, and scene furniture layout strictly follow reference images; no self-added or modified details; character proportions consistent throughout. Single-panel image — no outer border; no panel dividing line. Scene fingerprint: ornate marble fireplace + oval gold-framed portrait above fireplace + deep red carpet, position and form unchanged. Housekeeper: deep brown pinned hair must not become loose or light; white apron must not be removed; dark dress must not change to light. Catherine (aristocratic period): light cream dress must not change to dark; hair must not become disheveled; not adult tall proportions in cloud bubble. Proportion anchor: housekeeper seated with chair arm at elbow height; cloud-bubble Catherine at smaller inset scale. No floating text, no narration text, no title text, no label text in the image.

covered_sentences: She won the admiration of Isabella and the heart and soul of Edgar.
This pleased her very well, so she was very ambitious.
At Thrushcross Grange, where she had heard Heathcliff described as a vulgar ruffian and worse than an animal, she was careful not to act like him.
But at home, she did not bother to be polite or to restrain her wild nature.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 1:1 (equal narrative weight: Catherine at Thrushcross Grange vs Catherine at home). Left panel: camera positioned at the right side of the Thrushcross Grange drawing room, angled 30 degrees left-of-front, medium shot; the ornate marble fireplace is in the background; Catherine stands at center with Edgar to her right and Isabella to her left, all in the drawing room. Right panel: camera positioned at the right side of the Wuthering Heights main hall, angled 30 degrees right-of-front, low-angle slight upward tilt; the large stone-framed fireplace with iron grate is in the background; Catherine strides freely across the pale stone floor with Heathcliff visible in the right background watching her. Per-panel budget: left 3 recognizable (Catherine + Edgar + Isabella), right 2 recognizable (Catherine + Heathcliff); building element anchor present in both panels.

Left panel: Catherine stands center in the Thrushcross Grange drawing room, posture composed and graceful, a self-satisfied pleased smile on her face; Isabella stands to her left slightly behind, expression admiring and bright; Edgar stands to Catherine's right, expression warm and devoted; a crown-and-heart sketch icon floats above the trio indicating Catherine's social triumph. Right panel: Catherine strides across the Wuthering Heights stone floor with a released open expression — more natural and wild; Heathcliff leans against the stone wall in the right background at distance, watching; Catherine's thought bubble contains a silhouette sketch of a rough figure with a cross beside it (what the Lintons said about Heathcliff) with a matching arrow pointing away from herself — she is actively not being like him here.

Left panel: Thrushcross Grange drawing room, daytime; ornate marble fireplace in the background at adult waist height, deep red carpet underfoot, glass chandelier partially overhead; door frame visible at the frame edge clearly above all characters' heads; all three characters standing on the carpet in the center zone. Right panel: Wuthering Heights main hall, daytime; large stone-framed fireplace with iron grate in the background, pale stone floor underfoot, raw timber ceiling beams with hanging cured meats overhead; Heathcliff against the right stone wall in the background.

Left panel: warm bright drawing room daylight, lighter warm palette; right panel: stone hall ambient diffuse light, slightly cooler and earthier. The color temperature contrast between refined Thrushcross Grange (warm cream-gold) and Wuthering Heights (cool stone-grey) reinforces Catherine's double life.

Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic period): dark brown mid-length neatly arranged hair, slender figure, light cream fine dress with delicate pleats reaching ankle; in both panels her head top is clearly below the door frame and fireplace arch respectively by approximately two head-heights. Edgar Linton: light golden long curly hair, large calm light-blue eyes, refined face, light-colored gentleman coat; standing to Catherine's right in left panel at approximately the same height as her. Isabella Linton: golden or light brown hair, blue eyes, fair skin, slender delicate frame, light fine dress reaching ankle; standing to Catherine's left in left panel at approximately the same height as Catherine. Heathcliff: dark brown skin, black short slightly disheveled hair, sharp dark eyes, tall solid frame, dark coat; standing in right background of right panel — his head top clearly above Catherine's by approximately one full head. Full-room multi-person anchor: door frame in left panel clearly above all characters' heads; stone fireplace arch in right panel clearly above Catherine's head; Heathcliff clearly taller than Catherine. Proportion anchor: all three characters in left panel heads clearly below door frame; Heathcliff's head top above Catherine's by one full head in right panel.

Character appearance, clothing details, and scene furniture layout strictly follow reference images; no self-added or modified details; character proportions consistent throughout. White thin straight dividing line between panels, no rounded corners, no shadow, no gradient, no gap; no outer border. Left panel scene fingerprint: ornate marble fireplace + deep red carpet + glass chandelier, position and form unchanged. Right panel scene fingerprint: large stone-framed fireplace with iron grate + oak dresser with tin plates + pale stone floor, position and form unchanged. Catherine (aristocratic period): light cream dress must not change to dark; hair must not become disheveled; not adult tall proportions. covered_sentences: Mr. Edgar did not dare come to Wuthering Heights often; he was afraid of Hindley's drunken rages.
She did not like to see her two friends together.
If, when Linton was absent, Heathcliff expressed contempt for him, Catherine could seem to agree.
Similarly, if Linton showed disgust for Heathcliff in Heathcliff's absence, she could pretend to be indifferent.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Three-panel horizontal sequence, equal width thirds. Left panel: camera from outside the Wuthering Heights entrance, angled inward at 45 degrees, mild downward tilt; Edgar stands at the stone threshold in hesitant half-step, hat held at his waist with both hands, glancing nervously inward; the stone entrance door frame fills the left foreground, the courtyard stones are visible behind him. Center panel: camera at medium range from slightly above, angled 20 degrees left-of-front, looking toward Catherine and Heathcliff in the stone hall; no Edgar present; the stone fireplace arch is in the background. Right panel: camera at medium range from slightly above, angled 20 degrees right-of-front, looking toward Catherine and Edgar in the stone hall; no Heathcliff present; the stone fireplace arch is in the background. Per-panel budget: left 1 recognizable (Edgar at threshold), center 2 recognizable (Catherine + Heathcliff), right 2 recognizable (Catherine + Edgar).

Left panel: Edgar stands at the stone entrance threshold, hat in both hands at waist level, posture anxious and tentative, gaze directed inward with an expression of nervousness — his hesitant body language communicates his fear of Hindley's drunken rages without requiring text; no speech bubble. Center panel: Catherine stands facing slightly toward Heathcliff who stands to her right; his expression shows contempt directed off-panel toward the absent Edgar, brows furrowed; Catherine's expression subtly aligns with Heathcliff — a hint of agreement on her face; her thought bubble contains a simple Edgar Linton mini-icon (light curly hair head sketch) with a dismissive downward arrow beside it. Right panel: Catherine stands facing slightly toward Edgar who stands to her left; Edgar's expression shows mild disgust directed off-panel toward the absent Heathcliff; Catherine's expression is carefully blank, a mask of indifference; her thought bubble contains a simple Heathcliff mini-icon (dark hair head sketch) with a neutral dash beside it.

Wuthering Heights main hall, daytime; left panel: stone entrance door frame and courtyard visible, interior stone wall and pale floor glimpsed; center and right panels: large stone-framed fireplace with iron grate in the background, pale stone floor underfoot, raw timber ceiling beams with hanging cured meats overhead; oak dresser with tin plates in the distant background.

Daytime neutral stone hall light throughout all three panels; slightly warmer stone-light in center panel when Heathcliff and Catherine share the frame; slightly cooler and more formal in right panel with Edgar's presence.

Edgar Linton: light golden long curly hair, large calm light-blue eyes, refined elegant face, light-colored gentleman coat, white shirt; at the threshold in left panel — his head top below the upper edge of the stone door frame clearly. Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic period): dark brown mid-length neatly arranged hair, slender figure, light cream fine dress with delicate pleats; in center and right panels — stone fireplace arch clearly above her head; Heathcliff's head clearly higher than hers in the center panel, Edgar's head approximately level with hers in the right panel. Heathcliff: dark brown skin, black short slightly disheveled hair, sharp dark eyes, tall solid frame, dark coat; in center panel to Catherine's right — his head top clearly above Catherine's by approximately one full head, stone fireplace arch just above his head. Proportion anchor: Edgar head clearly below stone door frame upper edge in left panel; Heathcliff one full head taller than Catherine in center panel; Edgar approximately level with Catherine in right panel.

covered_sentences: She could use neither of these tricks when both were present.
One day Hindley was away, so Heathcliff decided to take a day off work.
He asked Cathy to spend the day with them, but she said she was expecting Edgar Linton.
Heathcliff went off on his own, feeling angry, hurt, and jealous.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 1:2 (right panel larger — Heathcliff's emotional departure is the heavier narrative weight; left panel shows the brief request-and-rejection exchange). Left panel: camera at medium range, angled 20 degrees left-of-front, looking toward Heathcliff and Catherine facing each other in the center of the hall; the stone fireplace arch is visible in the background. Right panel: camera at medium-close range from a low angle slightly behind and to the right of Heathcliff, tracking him walking away toward the main entrance in a three-quarter back view; the stone entrance door frame is in the background; Catherine is visible as a smaller figure standing still in the far left background of this panel. Per-panel budget: left 2 recognizable (Heathcliff + Catherine), right 1 recognizable main figure (Heathcliff in three-quarter back view); Catherine as smaller distant background figure in right panel.

Left panel: Heathcliff stands facing Catherine, posture relatively upright but beginning to stiffen, expression questioning then darkening as he hears her response; his speech bubble contains a sun-and-two-figure sketch (representing spending the day together). Catherine stands facing him, expression apologetic but firm, one hand slightly extended; her speech bubble contains an Edgar Linton mini-icon head sketch (light curly hair male head) with a clock/hourglass sketch beside it — she is expecting Edgar. Right panel: Heathcliff walks away in three-quarter back view toward the stone entrance, shoulders slightly hunched, pace heavy and deliberate; three simple emotion-sketch icons float above him: a clenched fist sketch (anger), a teardrop sketch (hurt), and a cracked-heart sketch (jealousy) arranged in a horizontal row — absorbing his three emotional states without text; Catherine appears as a smaller, still figure in the far left background near the fireplace, watching him leave.

Wuthering Heights main hall, daytime; left panel: stone fireplace with iron grate visible in the background, pale stone floor underfoot, raw timber ceiling beams with hanging cured meats overhead; Heathcliff and Catherine in the center zone, stone fireplace arch clearly above both their heads with Heathcliff's head notably closer to the arch than Catherine's. Right panel: stone main entrance door frame visible in the background as Heathcliff's destination; pale stone floor shows his movement path; Catherine in far left background near the fireplace zone, small in scale.

Daytime neutral stone hall light; left panel flat even daylight amplifying the awkwardness of the exchange; right panel slightly darker, the grey stone doorway framing his departure with a colder exit tone, creating a mood shift from the left panel.

Heathcliff: dark brown skin, black short slightly disheveled hair, sharp dark eyes, tall solid frame, dark coat, white shirt visible at collar; in left panel facing Catherine — his head top clearly above Catherine's by approximately one full head; in right panel three-quarter back view walking away — his tall frame occupies approximately two-thirds of the right panel height, the stone door frame top edge at approximately his head height. Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic period): dark brown mid-length neatly arranged hair, slender figure, light cream fine dress with delicate pleats reaching ankle; in left panel facing Heathcliff — her head at approximately two-thirds of Heathcliff's head height; in right panel she is a smaller background figure near the fireplace at approximately one-third of the panel height. Edgar Linton: not physically present — represented only by the mini-icon sketch in Catherine's speech bubble. Proportion anchor: Heathcliff one full head taller than Catherine in left panel; Heathcliff's head level with stone door frame top edge in right panel; Catherine in right panel background at approximately one-third panel height.
